summary: Matched several small previously-undecompiled functions across stage and item code: Big Blue Route setup forwarding, two Corneria animation helpers, a Kirby item distance-squared helper, Samus grapple pickup logic, and Whispy Apple velocity reset/state transition logic. The PR also tightened prototypes in headers for grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 and it_802F23AC. Reviewer feedback mainly pushed removal of unnecessary HSD_GObj casts when the typed gobj parameter was already acceptable.
what_changed: Implemented grBigBlueRoute_8020C1DC as a short wrapper that calls grBigBlueRoute_8020C85C() and then grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20(gobj).
Updated grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 prototype from UNK_PARAMS to Ground_GObj* in grbigblueroute.h.
Implemented grCorneria_801DD620 to call grAnime_801C8138 with the ground object's map_id.
Implemented grCorneria_801DFBF0 similarly, storing GET_GROUND(gobj) in a local Ground* before calling grAnime_801C8138.
Implemented it_802F23AC in itkirby_2F23.c as a distance-squared calculation between position data reached via obj + 0x2C and a float position array.
Updated it_802F23AC declaration in itkirby_2F23.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to double it_802F23AC(int* obj, float* pos).
Implemented it_2725_Logic53_PickedUp for Samus grapple to pad the stack, call Item_80268E5C with animation update mode 0, then call it_802A2428(gobj).
Implemented it_802EE6A0 for Whispy Apple to zero x/y/z velocity via GET_ITEM(gobj)->x40_vel and call Item_80268E5C with animation update mode 1.
Reordered includes in itwhispyapple.c so it/inlines.h appears before item subsystem headers.
smart_moves: Used narrow header prototype updates where function signatures became known, improving type information without broad unrelated churn.
For tiny stage helper matches, reused existing stage animation helper grAnime_801C8138 and GET_GROUND(gobj)->map_id instead of inventing new behavior.
For item logic functions, reused existing item helpers Item_80268E5C, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, GET_ITEM, and it_802A2428, matching project idioms for item state and animation transitions.

Kept the Kirby helper as a direct offset-based implementation because the surrounding type information was apparently still incomplete; comments documented the load/floating-point operation mapping from assembly.
Used PAD_STACK(16) in it_2725_Logic53_PickedUp, indicating attention to stack shape for matching a small function.
decomp_lessons: Small wrapper functions are often matchable by identifying the exact callee sequence and then updating only the directly affected prototype.

When a typed alias like Ground_GObj* or Item_GObj* is accepted by a helper, prefer passing the typed gobj directly rather than adding explicit HSD_GObj* casts unless needed for matching or compiler typing.
For item velocity reset functions, assigning a single local zero value to x40_vel.x/y/z can express the intended all-axis zeroing while preserving potential matching behavior.
Distance-squared helpers may return double even when all intermediate values are float; preserve the observed return type rather than simplifying to float without evidence.
If struct recovery is incomplete, temporary raw pointer arithmetic can be acceptable for matching, but the raw offsets should be isolated and documented for future cleanup.
naming_conventions: Stage functions retain address-style names such as grBigBlueRoute_8020C1DC and grCorneria_801DFBF0 until semantic names are known.
Item logic names combine item id or module address with behavior suffixes, e.g. it_2725_Logic53_PickedUp and itWhispyapple_UnkMotion1_Anim.
Project macros and type aliases use established uppercase helper forms such as GET_GROUND, GET_ITEM, PAD_STACK, and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE.
Header declarations keep address comments such as /* 20CD20 */ and /* 2F23AC */ alongside prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched grBigBlueRoute_8020C1DC as a two-call wrapper with no additional control flow.
Matched Corneria animation helpers by passing map_id from the Ground object into grAnime_801C8138 with third argument 0.
Matched it_802F23AC by preserving float temporaries dx/dy/dz, squaring each, summing, and returning as double; inline comments map source expressions to floating-point instructions.
Used PAD_STACK(16) in the Samus grapple pickup function to preserve stack layout.
Used a local f32 zero value in it_802EE6A0 before assigning all three velocity components, likely to influence generated code shape.
Reviewer suggestions imply that avoiding unnecessary explicit casts can still match and produce cleaner C in these cases.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested calling grAnime_801C8138(gobj, gp->map_id, 0) instead of casting gobj to HSD_GObj* in grCorneria_801DFBF0.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested Item_80268E5C(gobj, 1, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) instead of casting gobj to HSD_GObj*.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested Item_80268E5C(gobj, 0, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) instead of casting gobj to HSD_GObj*.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested grAnime_801C8138(gobj, GET_GROUND(gobj)->map_id, 0) for grCorneria_801DD620, again avoiding the explicit HSD_GObj* cast.

follow_up_queries: Search for other small grAnime_801C8138 wrappers that can be matched using GET_GROUND(gobj)->map_id.
Audit remaining Item_80268E5C call sites for unnecessary HSD_GObj* casts after typedef compatibility is known.
Investigate the real struct type behind it_802F23AC's int* obj and offset 0x2C to replace raw pointer arithmetic when safe.
Look for similar item velocity reset functions that zero x40_vel before calling Item_80268E5C.
Check whether grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 can now be further typed or decompiled given its Ground_GObj* prototype.
